name: Valkey (Redis-compatible Open Source Key-Value Store)
pricing_notes: Open source (BSD-3-Clause); Linux Foundation / valkey-io community

auth.notes: Redis-compatible AUTH; ACL (Access Control Lists) supported

performance.notes: Redis-compatible performance; multi-threaded I/O in Valkey 8

agent_readiness.notes: Redis-compatible open-source key-value store forked after Redis relicensing. Client: use redis-py or valkey-py (drop-in replacement). import valkey; r = valkey.Valkey(host='localhost', port=6379). r.set('key', 'value'). r.get('key'). r.hset('hash', 'field', 'value'). r.lpush('list', 'item'). r.sadd('set', 'member'). r.zadd('zset', {'member': 1.0}). r.expire('key', 300). Pipeline: pipe = r.pipeline(); pipe.set('a', 1); pipe.get('b'); pipe.execute(). Pub/sub: r.pubsub().subscribe('channel'). Streams: r.xadd('stream', {'field': 'value'}). Cluster support. Async: valkey.asyncio.Valkey(). All Redis commands supported. Used as drop-in Redis replacement.

gotchas: Ecosystem still young — fewer managed cloud offerings than Redis
valkey-py client is recommended over redis-py for long-term compatibility
Some Redis 7.x features may diverge over time as projects evolve separately
Cloud-managed Valkey options limited vs Redis Cloud, Elasticache
Migration from Redis is straightforward but requires testing
best_when: Redis replacement avoiding SSPL license restrictions
When Redis Cloud cost is prohibitive and self-hosting is viable
AWS ElastiCache (Valkey) or Google Memorystore (Valkey) managed options
avoid_when: When Redis's enterprise features (Redis Stack, modules) are required
Managed cloud where Redis is already established and working
Teams unfamiliar with Redis operational concerns
summary: Valkey is a Redis-compatible BSD-3-Clause key-value store (Linux Foundation fork after Redis relicensing). Use valkey-py or redis-py client. valkey.Valkey(host, port). set/get, hset, lpush, zadd, expire. Pipeline, pub/sub, streams. Cluster support. Async via valkey.asyncio. Performance comparable to Redis. Best for teams avoiding SSPL/Redis license. Managed via AWS Elasticache for Valkey or Google Memorystore.
